summary_one_line: A well-formed NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute value reaching valid.c:3807 with a non-zero trailing char, where corpus-energy allocation (not byte content) gates discovery.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD attribute declaration whose value is parsed as NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION (xmlValidateAttributeValueInternal cases XML_ATTRIBUTE_NMTOKENS/XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION at valid.c:3875) so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked, AND whose value string, after the name-char and 0x20-blank loops, ends on a non-zero character so that valid.c:3807 `if (val != 0)` evaluates TRUE. The required input is structural (valid XML+DTD prefix with an ENUMERATION/NMTOKENS attribute), not a specific magic literal; both arms share the same XML/DTD scaffold bytes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Minimizer reaches the blocker's true side (L3807 W T=12 F=0) and resolves it in 9/10 trials. Its calibrated power schedule (PowerSchedule None, intrinsic-calibration-only energy) keeps allocating havoc/token mutations to the seeds carrying the XML+DTD ENUMERATION scaffold, so it eventually mutates a value that lands val!=0 at line 3807.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Fast is byte-for-byte the same fuzzer except for the AFLFast FAST power schedule; it blocks 8/10 (loser blocked=8, L3807 L T=0 F=90 — never takes the true side). The FAST n_fuzz-based energy formula damps seeds whose coverage bucket has been hit campaign-wide, starving the deep attribute-validation path of mutation budget before val!=0 is produced.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by minimizer's calibrated energy schedule (PowerSchedule None: per-seed mutation budget derived only from intrinsic calibration perf_score). The loser fast applies the AFLFast n_fuzz rarity damping that throttles already-exercised buckets, so the single-technique delta (aflfast_rarity) costs fast the mutation budget needed to push a seed to val!=0 at valid.c:3807. This is a scheduling/energy divergence, not comparison-solving or byte-content.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the true branch of valid.c:3807 `if (val != 0) return(0)`.
The enclosing validator is invoked only for NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute types via the caller switch.
Both winner and loser seeds carry the same XML+DTD scaffold (version/DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtds prefix), so the differentiating feature is not a magic literal.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3807  T=12 F=0  T=0 F=90  if (val != 0) return(0);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Minimizer resolves the branch in 9/10 trials and fast blocks in 8/10.
Fast is identical to minimizer except for the FAST power schedule; the single-technique delta is the scheduler.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: The FAST schedule reweights per-seed mutation budget by n_fuzz, damping saturated paths and boosting rare ones.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Rarely-hit
paths get up to 4× energy; saturated paths are damped to 0.4×.

evidence_trail.claim: Minimizer's energy uses only intrinsic calibration and is not weighted by region hit-frequency.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is 'calibrated energy' (the minimizer/fast power-schedule axis), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage check does not apply. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms minimizer/fast emit only havoc/token mutation_op names with no dash rows, so no I2S-floor signal is expected; the divergence is attributable to the AFLFast n_fuzz energy damping in fast versus minimizer's intrinsic-calibration-only schedule, evidenced by identical-scaffold seeds and the L3807 T=12/T=0 split.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating valid.c:3807 reached via the ENUMERATION/NMTOKENS path where fast resolves the branch as often as minimizer across trials, showing the AFLFast rarity damping does not starve this path and that the divergence was byte-content (not energy) driven.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the FAST n_fuzz damping to this specific deep attribute-validation branch is inferred from the scheduler description plus shared-scaffold seeds; no per-seed n_fuzz/energy trace is present in the prompt to prove the path was actually starved rather than blocked by some byte feature I cannot see in the truncated diff.
